title: Brain Development from 0 to 3 Years Old ~ Inside a Baby's Head ~
description: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!

scenes.speech: Let's uncover the secrets of a baby's brain together!
scenes.title: Brain Development from 0 to 3 Years Old ~ Inside a Baby's Head ~

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: A Baby's Brain is More Complex than the Universe!
scenes.content: At birth, a baby's brain already has 100 billion neurons! By age 3, over 100 trillion synapses (neural connections) are formed, creating more connections than the number of stars in the universe.
scenes.highlight: Baby's brain neurons: 100 billion. Synaptic connections: over 100 trillion by age 3!

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Understanding the Critical Period for Language Development
scenes.content: There is a 'critical period' for language acquisition. Exposing children to multiple languages before age 3 can help them achieve native-level proficiency! This is the scientific basis for bilingual education in early childhood.
scenes.highlight: Bilingual education: Starting before age 3 can lead to native-level proficiency in a second language

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The Relationship Between Music and the Brain
scenes.content: Listening to and playing music activates a wide range of brain areas. Children who start playing instruments between ages 3-5 tend to have better language skills, math skills, and attention. While the 'Mozart Effect' is not proven, the benefits of music education are scientifically verified!
scenes.highlight: Instrument-playing children: Higher scores in language, memory, and attention control tests compared to non-playing children (multiple studies)

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Sleep Nurtures the Brain
scenes.content: Growth hormone is released during sleep! Children aged 3 need 12-14 hours of sleep, while elementary school kids need 10-11 hours. Naps are also important for memory consolidation. Staying up late with smartphones and games can significantly damage a child's brain development!
scenes.highlight: Growth hormone: Released 90% during sleep (especially during non-REM sleep)

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Developing Emotional Regulation (EQ)
scenes.content: EQ (emotional intelligence) predicts long-term success in life, not just IQ! Emotional regulation, or the ability to control anger, anxiety, and sadness, develops between ages 3-6. Parents can support this by labeling and validating their child's emotions.
scenes.highlight: EQ vs. IQ: EQ has a greater impact on long-term success in life (Goleman research)

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The Worst Impact on the Brain: Chronic Stress
scenes.content: Chronic stress (abuse, poverty, family conflict) can cause physical damage to a child's brain, especially the amygdala and prefrontal cortex. A safe and secure home environment is essential for brain development.
scenes.highlight: Children with chronic stress: Cortisol (stress hormone) shrinks the hippocampus

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The Relationship Between Nutrition and Brain Development
scenes.content: The brain accounts for only 2% of body weight but consumes 20% of energy. Breast milk, DHA, iron, and vitamin D support brain development. Children with picky eating habits often lack essential nutrients!
scenes.highlight: DHA (fish oil): Main component of brain neural membranes. Multiple studies show improved cognitive function in children with DHA intake

scenes.speech: Let's learn about brain development with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The Effects of Nature Experience on the Brain
scenes.content: Outdoor play and nature experiences foster curiosity, risk judgment, motor skills, immune function, and creativity. With the rise of 'nature deficit disorder,' making time for outdoor play is crucial!
scenes.highlight: Over 2 hours of nature experience per week: Significant improvements in health, well-being, and creativity (international research)

scenes.speech: The secrets of a baby's brain revealed! Next stop: The Science of Play 🎉
scenes.title: Lesson Completed!
